title: Daily Market Digest: May 5, 2026
subtitle: Market navigating geopolitical tensions and strong Q1 earnings

items.headline: U.S. Futures Edge Higher as Markets Weigh Geopolitical Risks Against Robust Earnings
items.summary: U.S. stock futures showed modest gains this morning, with the S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow futures all pointing upward, despite a flare-up in tensions between the U.S. and Iran near the Strait of Hormuz. This follows a mixed close on Monday, where major indices declined amid geopolitical fears. The market is currently balancing renewed concerns over Middle East stability and elevated oil prices with a stronger-than-expected Q1 2026 earnings season. Strong corporate profitability, particularly in technology and consumer discretionary, continues to provide underlying support, with blended earnings growth for the S&P 500 estimated around 27% year-over-year. However, some analysts note a lack of broad market participation in recent rallies, signaling caution. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Pinterest Soars on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Pinterest ($PINS) surged 17.5% in premarket trading after reporting first-quarter sales and profit that topped Wall Street estimates, driven by strong monthly active user growth.

items.headline: Anheuser-Busch Inbev Jumps on Forecast Beat
items.summary: Anheuser-Busch Inbev ($ABIBEV) shares climbed over 6% in premarket trading after the Budweiser maker exceeded analyst sales and profit forecasts due to strong beer sales.

items.headline: GameStop Slips After eBay Bid
items.summary: GameStop ($GME) was down slightly in premarket, continuing its slide from Monday's 10% drop, following its proposal to acquire online marketplace eBay for approximately $56 billion in cash and stock.

items.headline: Shuttle Pharmaceuticals Holdings Declines
items.summary: Shuttle Pharmaceuticals Holdings, Inc. ($SHPH) experienced a significant decline of 32.84%, making it one of the biggest losers in early May 2026.

items.headline: U.S. Trade Deficit Widens in March 2026
items.summary: The U.S. goods and services deficit increased to $60.3 billion in March, marking a $2.5 billion rise from February's revised figure. March exports were $320.9 billion, and imports were $381.2 billion.

items.headline: New York Fed President Sees No Immediate Need for Rate Hikes
items.summary: The New York Federal Reserve President indicated today that there is currently no need to consider raising interest rates, though the future direction of rates remains uncertain.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Continue to Affect Oil Markets
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East escalated with reports of an exchange of fire between the U.S. and Iran near the Strait of Hormuz. While oil prices are easing slightly today, they remain elevated, with Brent crude at $113.30 per barrel and U.S. crude at $104.58 per barrel.

items.headline: Q1 2026 Earnings Season Exceeding Expectations
items.summary: The Q1 2026 earnings season is proving largely positive, with over 60% of S&P 500 companies reporting results better than anticipated. Blended earnings growth for the S&P 500 is estimated at approximately 27% year-over-year. Notably, American Electric Power ($AEP), PayPal Holdings ($PYPL), and Ball Corporation ($BALL) all reported strong Q1 results today, reaffirming positive outlooks and guidance.

items.headline: AI Demand Fuels Growth in Technology and Durable Goods
items.summary: Demand for AI infrastructure continues to surge, with cloud compute platforms experiencing rising utilization. This trend is reflected in economic data, with durable orders up 0.8% in March, primarily driven by a 3.6% jump in computers and electronics, supported by strong AI and data-center demand. The Communication Services, Information Technology, and Consumer Discretionary sectors are currently leading the market.
